Myrtlesque Returns!
Friday 10th April @theoak.tigheshill from 8pm
Support: National Radio

Friday 17th April @sevenseashotel_ from 7:30pm
Note: @myrtlesque will play two sets between 8-10pm

@myrtlesque @theoak.tigheshill 
Abandoned Work of Art Tour continues! Saturday 6th September
Bistro from 6:30pm, Show at 8:30pm
Poster design by @fearonquothing 

Abandoned Work of Art is the opening track of Myrtlesqueâs long gestating rock opera, a deathbed fairytaleâ¦ âA Dreamerâs Eyesâ, currently in the making with four new songs in progress with @sean_ballenden at @triple_three_records Maitland.

Supported by National Radio.

Lyric excerpt
âDonât you tell me where to start 
With my abandoned work of art
And all I ever knew was holding on for dear life
But what is stifling me but a swallowed dream?
If Iâm holding on to that work of art
What if I never share it?
I never dare it, and who fucking cares about it?
No oneâ¦ no one but meâ¦

But I feel in myself a perception shift, I still donât know what to do with it ð«â

The Abandoned Work of Art Tour will showcase the songs, the stories and the inspiration from Myrtlesque, the evocative musical project of Matt Petherbridge, a Maitland NSW based singer-songwriter and storyteller.

âAbandonedâ¦ is a rare song. It captures my inner psychology of my own career, and whatâs possible next and it taps into the story of Myrtlesque and the rock opera âA Dreamerâs Eyesâ. To dare to share something so personal may prove to be universal. I trust it will open the right doors for my work right across the worldâ, says Petherbridge.

Blending intricate lyricism with rich, atmospheric songs rooted in themes of parenthood, memory, and the passage of time, Myrtlesque crafts songs at the crossroads of folk and alternative music that feel both deeply personal and universally resonant.

All press enquiries via myrtlesque@gmail.com
